title: coreopsis macbridei
intro: Coreopsis macbridei, also known as the Peruvian coreopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to Peru and other parts of South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is valued for its ability to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Coreopsis macbridei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis macbridei may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
